chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-4886 of 2021 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. For hearing of CMA No.20067/2021 (Stay/A) 2. For hearing of main case -------------------------------------------------------------- 24.09.2021. Mr. Arshad Siraj, advocate for the petitioner. >><< 1&2. Learned counsel states that the facts of the instant petition are similar as that of petition bearing CP No.D-3381 of 2017, which has already been disposed of vide order dated 06.06.2017. He also provided a copy of the Order to the Court. He, therefore, requests that the instant petition may be disposed of in the same terms as decided in the above referred petition. Having gone through the aforesaid order, we find ourselves in agreement with the submissions made by the learned counsel for the petitioner. Consequently, the instant petition is disposed of in the same terms as decided in the above referred petition, which is as under: “....dispose of this petition with the directions to the respondents not to enforce the recovery of the impugned demand, which is the subject matter of the appeal in the instant case before the Commissioner (Appeals-II), Inland Revenue, Karachi till its final decision. In case of any adverse order, if passed by the Commissioner (Appeals-II) against the petitioner, the respondents shall not enforce the recovery of the impugned demand for another period of seven days from the date of receipt of such Appellate order to enable the petitioner to seek further remedy in accordance with law.

Consequently, the instant petition is disposed of in the same terms as decided in the above referred petition, which is as under: “....dispose of this petition with the directions to the respondents not to enforce the recovery of the impugned demand, which is the subject matter of the appeal in the instant case before the Commissioner (Appeals-II), Inland Revenue, Karachi till its final decision. In case of any adverse order, if passed by the Commissioner (Appeals-II) against the petitioner, the respondents shall not enforce the recovery of the impugned demand for another period of seven days from the date of receipt of such Appellate order to enable the petitioner to seek further remedy in accordance with law. The Commissioner (Appeals) may decide the appeal of the petitioner, preferably within four weeks from the date of receipt of this order, which shall be communicated to him by the petitioner within seven days from the date of this order.” Petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. JUDGE JUDGE Gulzar/PA
